video_title: Glazer Family Members Said to Study Manchester United Stake Sale
transcript: Now Bloomberg understands some members of the Glaser family, we're talking football, are debating whether to sell their stake in Manchester United Football Club after more than twenty years of ownership. Now with more details, Bloomberg's Tia Adebayo is here. Tia, we're all obsessed. We're football bad because of the World Cup. So there are well, we understand. So Bloomberg scooped our internal discussions. What do we actually know about the Glaser family? Well, that's right, Francine. Bloomberg exclusive today. There are these initial talks about whether or not to exit from Manchester United for the Glaser family. In terms of the family itself, they're worth about $10,000,000,000. They've got interest in other sports teams like the Tampa Bay Buccaneers. But these are just initial talks, and there are said to be some members of the family that are against this, and you'd need their support if a sale were to take place. But this is really crucial because that twenty year tenure for the Glaser family at Manchester United is one of the longest in English football history. This is also quite a crucial time for Manchester United. There's talks about upgrading the famous Old Trafford Stadium. That could cost billions of dollars, but it's also something that a big football club needs. We've seen that happen with the likes of Tottenham Hotspur recently. And, of course, we should mention, sorry, to any Man United fans. They've had some mixed performances recently. Got rid of a manager this season, so a crucial time for the club and for the company as well. So how much interest would there be if if these shares, you know, are offered for sale? Well, the last time, that there was a sale like this talked about in 1998, so we have the likes of Rupert Murdoch interested in Manchester United. So there's likely to be a lot of interest both on the Middle Eastern side. We've seen the success of the Qatari, sports investment arm of their sovereign wealth fund with PSG. They've had great success there. And, also, the likes of other wealthy US families, the Cronkeys at Arsenal have had again good success there. So we'll see quite a lot of interest, but it's likely to come at quite a steep price. Interest in m and a's like these has been growing recently. So whoever gets to own this club,
